content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from ICM/RND curious agent research) can be repurposed to prioritize which new experiences an active learning AI agent stores in its long-term memory, rather than just which unlabeled data points it queries for annotation. This creates a self-reinforcing memory loop where the agent grows both its predictive accuracy and memory knowledge base by chasing novel, information-dense experiences that align with its intrinsic curiosity.
